item.title: Large-Cap Segment Surges with Shriram Finance Leading Gains Amid Defensive and Cyclical Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, demonstrated robust performance with a 1.76% gain on 26 Mar 2026, extending its positive momentum over the past week with a 1.23% rise. Shriram Finance emerged as the standout performer, delivering a strong return of 5.69%, while Tech Mahindra lagged with a 1.66% decline. The advance-decline ratio further underscored the bullish sentiment, with 93 stocks advancing against only 7 declining, reflecting a healthy 13.29x ratio. Time: 32 minutes ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Surges with Strong Breadth and Sectoral Momentum
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated robust performance recently, advancing 2.41% on the day and gaining 1.14% over the past five trading sessions. This rally underscores renewed investor interest in smaller companies, supported by broad market participation and positive sectoral trends. Time: 32 minutes ago

item.title: Sensex and Nifty Rally as Small Caps Lead Market Gains; Broad-Based Sector Advances Mark Strong Momentum
item.description: Indian equity markets surged on 26 March 2026, with the Sensex climbing 1,205 points to close at 75,273.45, marking a 1.63% gain, while the Nifty 50 index advanced 394.05 points or 1.72% to settle at 23,306.45. The rally was broad-based, led by small caps and mid caps, as all 38 sectors on the BSE recorded gains, reflecting robust investor appetite amid positive global cues and sustained domestic buying. Time: 32 minutes ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Surges 2.34% Led by Strong Breadth and Sectoral Gains
item.description: The mid-cap segment demonstrated robust performance on 26 Mar 2026, with the BSE Midcap 150 index rising by 2.34%, continuing its upward momentum over the past week with a 1.55% gain. This surge was supported by strong breadth, sectoral contributions, and notable upgrades in stock ratings, underscoring renewed investor confidence in mid-sized companies. Time: 32 minutes ago

item.title: Sensex and Nifty Surge as Small Caps Lead Market Rally; All Sectors Advance
item.description: Indian equity markets witnessed a robust rally on 26 March 2026, with the Sensex and Nifty posting strong gains amid broad-based sectoral advances. Small-cap stocks outperformed, driving the market higher as investor sentiment improved on positive domestic and global cues. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Surges with Strong Breadth and Sectoral Momentum
item.description: The small-cap segment, as measured by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated robust performance recently, advancing 2.41% on the day and gaining 1.14% over the past five trading sessions. This rally underscores renewed investor interest in smaller companies, supported by broad market participation and positive sectoral trends. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Surges 2.34% Led by Broad-Based Gains and Upgraded Stocks
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, demonstrated robust performance on 26 Mar 2026, advancing by 2.34% on the day and maintaining a solid 1.55% gain over the past five sessions. This rally was supported by broad market participation, with 142 stocks advancing against only 8 decliners, resulting in an impressive advance-decline ratio of 17.75x. Sectoral contributions and recent upgrades in stock ratings further bolstered investor sentiment in this space. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Surges 1.76% Led by Shriram Finance; Tech Mahindra Lags
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, has demonstrated robust performance with a 1.76% gain on the day and a steady 1.23% rise over the past five sessions. This upward momentum is underpinned by a strong advance-decline ratio and notable divergences between defensive and cyclical stocks, signalling nuanced investor sentiment amid evolving market conditions. Time: 1 hour ago
